bullets.text: Hurricane Ian made multiple Florida landfalls in late September, generating an estimated $1.4 billion direct Property loss; reinsurance capped retained losses at $200 million (36 points on Q3 Property CR) and ~$290 million in special lines including 60% from boat losses.
Companywide Q3 CR was 99.2, bringing YTD to 96.5, just above the 96 target; catastrophe losses contributed 7.1 points to the quarter including 3.1 points from Hurricane Ian in Personal Lines.

Excluding catastrophe impacts, underlying personal auto severity continued to rise due to economic inflation and supply chain pressures, though calendar-year frequency stabilized and ran below pre-pandemic levels.

Progressive raised personal auto rates in 20 states in Q3, averaging about 2% countrywide, bringing YTD rate increases to nearly 12%; prior rate increases from 2021 and early 2022 continued to earn in, improving underlying profitability.

Total auto PIF declined 1% year over year but grew by nearly 250,000 since June; new auto applications rose 20% in both channels as rate competitiveness improved versus peers who were also raising rates.
Companywide NPW grew 5% for the quarter and 8% YTD; Personal Lines NPW grew 9% while Commercial Lines NPW declined 13% primarily due to timing shifts in TNC policy renewals.

Commercial auto 8.0 product and new policy administration system elevated to three additional states, reaching 33 states representing 80% of commercial auto premium; telematics premiums under Smart Haul and Snapshot ProView grew 20% YTD.

Investment portfolio returned -1.9% in Q3 as persistent inflation and Federal Reserve tightening drove fixed-income losses of -1.8% and equity losses of -4.5%; Progressive maintained a conservative posture awaiting better entry points.

Over 1,000 Progressive employees worked extra hours and 200 volunteered into other workgroups during the Ian response; an Insurance Village in Fort Myers provided food, water, advances, and direct claims support to displaced policyholders.
